lenses.guidance: Abraham buys the cave of Machpelah (vv 17-20) — his only land-holding in Canaan despite the promise that 'all this land' would be his (12:7, 13:15). Hebrews 11:13-16 reads the patriarchs' modest claims as evidence they were 'looking for a country of their own' beyond this earth.

The cave of Machpelah recurs at the end of Genesis: Abraham and Sarah, then Isaac, Rebekah, Leah, and Jacob are all buried there (49:30-31; 50:13). The plot echoes through the canon as the patriarchs' down payment on a promise the new creation will fully deliver (Heb 11:39-40).
